pattern: Only a running count of how many message words are exact banned-word hits matters, so a set lookup plus an early-exit counter settles the question the moment a second hit appears.
hints: Put every banned word into a set first so checking any single message word against the whole banned list is a single lookup.
Walk through the message words once, counting how many are found in the banned set.
You can stop the moment the count reaches two -- there's no need to keep scanning once spam is already confirmed.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, two banned words present
statement example, only one banned word present
single message word, single banned word, exact match
banned word repeated twice in the message
no overlap at all between message and banned words
banned word list longer than message but no matches

stress.description: random word lists with a controlled overlap chance
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    vocab = [''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(5)) for _ in range(20)]
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    message = [rng.choice(vocab) for _ in range(n)]
    m = rng.randint(1, 20)
    banned = [rng.choice(vocab) for _ in range(m)]
    return [message, banned]
